title: Opulaster tanakae
taxonomy: Opulaster tanakae belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is a species of flowering plant that is native to Japan. The plant is classified as a shrub, with a small to medium size, typically growing up to 1-2 meters in height. Opulaster tanakae is often found in mountainous regions and is known for its white, fragrant flowers that bloom in late summer. The specific classification of Opulaster tanakae is within the genus Opulaster, which is part of the Rosaceae family.
uses: Opulaster tanakae, also known as Tanaka's goldenrod, is a plant species with potential medicinal uses. Research suggests that it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. intro: Opulaster tanakae, also known as Tanaka's guelder rose or Japanese guelder rose, is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to East Asia. The health benefits of Opulaster tanakae include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Opulaster tanakae is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, or used to make a tincture, to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
